title: zlib zmemcpy — byte slice copy
signature: pub fn zmemcpy(dst: &mut [u8], src: &[u8], n: usize)
notes: Copy exactly n bytes from src to dst. Panics if either slice is shorter than n — matches C's memcpy which is UB on out-of-bounds. Safe Rust surfaces the bug loudly rather than silently truncating.

title: zlib zmemcmp — byte slice compare
signature: pub fn zmemcmp(s1: &[u8], s2: &[u8], n: usize) -> i32
notes: Compare exactly n bytes lexicographically. Panics if either slice is shorter than n — matches C memcmp's contract. Returns first-differing-byte signed difference; 0 iff all n bytes equal.

title: zlib zmemzero — zero exactly len bytes
notes: Zero exactly len bytes. Panics if buffer is shorter — matches C memset's UB contract. No silent truncation.

title: Reverse the byte order of a 64-bit integer
notes: Thin wrapper over u64::swap_bytes. The C version handles both 32- and 64-bit words via a macro; Rust's u64::swap_bytes covers both when a u32 is zero-extended.

title: Reverse the bits of a code value
signature: pub fn bi_reverse(code: u32, len: u8) -> u32
notes: Reverse the bottom `len` bits of `code`. Used when emitting Huffman codes in DEFLATE.
